message:
test(python): NetworkX conformance for duplication-family generators

New conformance test module covering franken_networkx's
partial_duplication_graph and duplication_divergence_graph against
the upstream networkx reference implementation.

The shape mirrors the established conformance test pattern in this
repo: drive both fnx.X(...) and nx.X(...) with identical seeds /
arguments, snapshot each via _graph_signature, and assert the
signatures match exactly.

_graph_signature(graph) captures the full identity surface:
- class name (Graph vs DiGraph vs MultiGraph etc)
- directed / multigraph flags
- graph-level attrs (graph.graph)
- node list as sorted (node, attrs) pairs
- edge list as sorted (u, v, attrs) triples

Sorting on nodes/edges is what makes parity assertions robust
against insertion-order differences between the two impls — both
networkx and franken_networkx are free to build the graph in any
order as long as the resulting set is identical.

Test shapes:

test_duplication_generators_seeded_output_matches_networkx
- partial_duplication_graph(8, 3, 0.5, 0.2, seed=4)
- partial_duplication_graph(6, 6, 0.5, 0.2, seed=7) — corner case
  where n == initial graph size
- duplication_divergence_graph(8, 0.5, seed=4)
- duplication_divergence_graph(2, 0.5, seed=7) — minimum useful n
- Each parametrized case runs identical args through fnx and nx;
  expects byte-for-byte signature equality.

test_duplication_generators_reject_unsupported_create_using
- Both fnx and nx are expected to raise NetworkXError when
  create_using is DiGraph or MultiGraph (these duplication
  generators only support undirected simple graphs).
- Cross-product (2 generators × 2 create_using values = 4 cases)
  ensures we cover both unsupported-graph-class branches in both
  generators.
- Both branches assert pytest.raises(NetworkXError) — error
  parity matters because callers downstream catch the error
  string and dispatch on it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,52 @@
+"""NetworkX conformance tests for duplication-family generators."""
+
+import pytest
+import networkx as nx
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+
+def _graph_signature(graph):
+    return {
+        "class": type(graph).__name__,
+        "directed": graph.is_directed(),
+        "multigraph": graph.is_multigraph(),
+        "graph": dict(graph.graph),
+        "nodes": sorted((node, dict(attrs)) for node, attrs in graph.nodes(data=True)),
+        "edges": sorted((u, v, dict(attrs)) for u, v, attrs in graph.edges(data=True)),
+    }
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    "name,args,kwargs",
+    [
+        ("partial_duplication_graph", (8, 3, 0.5, 0.2), {"seed": 4}),
+        ("partial_duplication_graph", (6, 6, 0.5, 0.2), {"seed": 7}),
+        ("duplication_divergence_graph", (8, 0.5), {"seed": 4}),
+        ("duplication_divergence_graph", (2, 0.5), {"seed": 7}),
+    ],
+)
+def test_duplication_generators_seeded_output_matches_networkx(name, args, kwargs):
+    actual = getattr(fnx, name)(*args, **kwargs)
+    expected = getattr(nx, name)(*args, **kwargs)
+
+    assert _graph_signature(actual) == _graph_signature(expected)
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    "name,args",
+    [
+        ("partial_duplication_graph", (8, 3, 0.5, 0.2)),
+        ("duplication_divergence_graph", (8, 0.5)),
+    ],
+)
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("create_using", [fnx.DiGraph, fnx.MultiGraph])
+def test_duplication_generators_reject_unsupported_create_using(name, args, create_using):
+    nx_create_using = nx.DiGraph if create_using is fnx.DiGraph else nx.MultiGraph
+
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError) as expected:
+        getattr(nx, name)(*args, seed=4, create_using=nx_create_using)
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError) as actual:
+        getattr(fnx, name)(*args, seed=4, create_using=create_using)
+
+    assert str(actual.value) == str(expected.value)
